DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANTS BRICK AND TIMBER, INC., SF MULTIFAMILY IV PROPERTY OWNER LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. THE COURT CONTINUES THE MATTER FOR HEARING TO JANUARY 7, 2026. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER FURTHER, BY TELEPHONE OR IN PERSON, WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER, AND TO MAKE GOOD FAITH EFFORTS TO RESOLVE THIS DISCOVERY DISPUTE. IF THE SUBPOENAS REMAIN IN DISPUTE, MOVING PARTY MAY PROVIDE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF BY NINE COURT DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE, AND PLAINTIFF MAY PROVIDE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF BY FIVE COURT DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE. : CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BRICK AND TIMBER, INC., and SF MULTIFAMILY IV PROPERTY OWNER LLC's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Micah A. Thompson, Esq., of Tenant Law Group, (888) 510-7511, for Plaintiff.
Eric J. Phillips, Esq., of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP, (415) 433-0990, for Defendants.

Not argued/contested. There is discussion regarding the above Motion to Quash and of the last-minute request to continue the matter. At the conclusion of discussion, the matter is continued to Jun-11-2026. Counsel is instructed to submit any update(s) to the court by Jun-04-2026. Counsel is admonished to submit requests to continue no later than three (3) days prior to hearing.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff Steve Lakovits' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set Two), Nos. 65 And 69, To Defendant Brick And Timber, Inc.; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF STEVE LAKOVITS', AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set Two), Nos. 65 And 69, To Defendant Brick And Timber, Inc.; And Request For Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Micah A. Thompson, Esq., of Tenant Law Group, (888) 510-7511, for Plaintiff.
Eric J. Phillips, Esq., of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP, (415) 433-0990, for Defendants.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to compel further responses to plaintiff Steve Lakovits' second set of special interrogatories, nos. 65 and 69, is granted. Defendant Brick and Timber is ordered to respond to the interrogatories consistent with the parties' meet and confer agreement (see Thompson Decl., para. 7) within two weeks. Defendant Brick and Timber's failure to amend consistent with its agreement lacks substantial justification. Defendant shall pay $2580 to Lakovits within 30 days.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff Steve Lakovits' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two (Nos. 22, 36, 48, 93, 94, 96, 99, 101, 102, And 139), To Defendant Brick And Timber, Inc. And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF STEVE LAKOVITS', AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two (Nos. 22, 36, 48, 93, 94, 96, 99, 101, 102, And 139), To Defendant Brick And Timber, Inc. And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Micah A. Thompson, Esq., of Tenant Law Group, (888) 510-7511, for Plaintiff.
Eric J. Phillips, Esq., of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP, (415) 433-0990, for Defendants.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to compel further responses to plaintiff Steve Lakovits' second set of requests for production of documents, numbers 22, 36, 48, 93, 94, 96, 99, 101, 102, and 139, is granted. Defendant Brick and Timber Inc.'s objections lack merit. Defendant shall serve further responses within two weeks and make full production within 30 days.

Lakovits seeks sanctions. The court concludes Brick and Timber's objections lack substantial justification but awards partial sanctions because the motion was unopposed. Brick and Timber shall pay $1920 to Lakovits within 30 days.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
